summary:
• Iranian sanctions relief prospects are creating downward price pressure on Western Hemisphere crude markets, with oil tumbling 4% on peace deal optimism and Trump signals potentially reshaping geopolitical supply dynamics.

• Russian supply disruptions from Ukrainian strikes are tightening global crude availability while simultaneously Iran normalization discussions create conflicting directional pressures on oil valuations and regional energy security.

• Iraq's energy sector faces critical operational tests amid broader Western Hemisphere infrastructure vulnerabilities, coinciding with Goldman Sachs' demand uncertainty concerns that could constrain crude pricing through 2027.
